segments.text: "These are the words of Amos, who was among the sheepherders[^1] of Tekoa—what he saw concerning Israel two years[^2] before the earthquake, in the days when Uzziah was king of Judah and Jeroboam son of Jehoash[^3] was king of Israel.

footnotes.text: + Hebrew Joash, a variant of Jehoash

segments.text: "He said:
The LORD roars from Zion and raises His voice from Jerusalem; the pastures of the shepherds mourn, and the summit of Carmel withers.
segments.speaker: He

segments.text: "This is what the LORD says:
For three transgressions of Damascus, even four, I will not revoke My judgment, [^4] because they threshed Gileadwith sledges of iron.
segments.speaker: the Lord

footnotes.text: + Or I will not revoke the punishment; Hebrew I will not revoke it; also in verses 6, 9, 11, and 13

segments.text: So I will send fire upon the house of Hazaelto consume the citadels of Ben-hadad.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I will break down the gates of Damascus; I will cut off the ruler[^5] of the Valley of Aven[^6] and the one who wields the scepter in Beth-eden. The people of Aram will be exiled to Kir,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: says the LORD.
"This is what the LORD says:
For three transgressions of Gaza, even four, I will not revoke My judgment, because they exiled a whole population, delivering them up to Edom.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So I will send fire upon the walls of Gaza, to consume its citadels.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I will cut off the ruler of Ashdod and the one who wields the scepter in Ashkelon. I will turn My hand against Ekron, and the remnant of the Philistines will perish,
segments.speaker: the Lord God

segments.text: says the Lord GOD.
"This is what the LORD says:
For three transgressions of Tyre, even four, I will not revoke My judgment, because they delivered up a whole congregation of exiles to Edom and broke a covenant of brotherhood.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So I will send fire upon the walls of Tyreto consume its citadels.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: "This is what the LORD says:

For three transgressions of Edom, even four, I will not revoke My judgment, because he pursued his brother with the sword and stifled all compassion; his anger raged continually, and his fury flamed incessantly.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So I will send fire upon Temanto consume the citadels of Bozrah.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: "This is what the LORD says:
For three transgressions of the Ammonites, even four, I will not revoke My judgment, because they ripped open the pregnant women of Gileadin order to enlarge their territory.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So I will kindle a fire in the walls of Rabbahto consume its citadelsamid war cries on the day of battle and a violent wind on the day of tempest.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Their king will go into exile[^7]—he and his princes together,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: says the LORD.

footnotes.text: + Possibly Milcom will go into exile; Milcom, also called Molech, was god of the Ammonites; see Leviticus 18:21 and 1 Kings 11:7.
